channel.title: CQL
channel.description: An XML representation of a search request

item.title: [SUPS25-154] Accessibility Remediation - Performance-Related Accessibility
item.description:
Issue: Lighthouse flagged a very large DOM (over 2,300 elements), unused CSS and JavaScript files, and images that are not lazy-loaded. These issues don’t directly fail WCAG, but they slow down assistive technologies and increase cognitive load for users.

WCAG References: 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable), 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks – indirectly affected)

Recommendation: Reduce the overall DOM size, clean up unused CSS/JS, and enable lazy-loading for non-critical images.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

item.title: [SUPS25-153] Accessibility Remediation - Code Quality & Markup
item.description:
Issue: There are duplicate IDs on multiple elements, content that falls outside of semantic landmarks, and some navigation elements are built with non-semantic markup.

WCAG References: 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks)

Recommendation: Make all IDs unique, use semantic HTML for lists and menus, and ensure all content is contained within proper landmarks such as <header>, <main>, <nav>, and <footer>.

comments.comment: Estimate: 12h

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

9/2/25, 11:36 AM (EST) - Scott Forden:

Estimate: 12h

item.title: [SUPS25-152] Accessibility Remediation - Forms & Interactive Elements
item.description:
Issue: Some form inputs are missing labels, programmatic purposes (such as autocomplete="email"), and submit buttons without proper names. In addition, interactive controls in the navigation are nested in a way that can break keyboard navigation and screen readers.

WCAG References: 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), 3.3.2 (Labels or Instructions), 2.1.1 (Keyboard)

Recommendation: Provide explicit labels or aria-labels for all inputs, use autocomplete attributes where applicable, and simplify interactive structures to avoid nested controls.

comments.comment: Estimate: 10h

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

9/2/25, 11:51 AM (EST) - Scott Forden:

Estimate: 10h

item.title: [SUPS25-151] Accessibility Remediation - Links
item.description:
Issue: Several links are either missing descriptive text (icon-only links, testimonial carousels) or are duplicated directly next to each other. In some cases, the same link text points to different destinations, which is confusing for screen reader users.

WCAG References: 2.4.4 (Link Purpose, In Context), 2.4.9 (Link Purpose, Link Only – AAA)

Recommendation: Add clear, descriptive text or aria-labels to every link, merge duplicate links, and ensure link text always matches its destination.

comments.comment: Estimate: 10h

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

9/2/25, 12:12 PM (EST) - Scott Forden:

Estimate: 10h

item.title: [SUPS25-150] Accessibility Remediation - Headings & Page Structure
item.description:
Issue: Headings are not in a logical order (e.g., multiple <h4> elements appearing before an <h2>), which makes it harder for screen reader users to understand the page outline.

WCAG References: 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels)

Recommendation: Reorganize heading levels so they follow a proper hierarchy (h1 → h2 → h3, etc.), and ensure all headings are descriptive.

comments.comment: Estimate: 10h

Status: Ready for review.

PR: https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/349

Theme: SUPS25-150 Ready for Production Accessibility: Headings

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180450099567
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147238322313
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=145085726902
Reviewed and approved. Ready for QA.

Re-tested on Preview sites

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180450099567
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147238322313
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=145085726902

Validation of the fixed issue:

  • Headings are not in a logical order (e.g., multiple <h4> elements appearing before an <h2>), which makes it harder for screen reader users to understand the page outline.

Test Case:

  1. Navigate to each site’s preview theme
  2. Observe all pages with a headings map tool to verify that the hierarchy of headings is accurate
    1. Some third-party pages cannot be adjusted on our end, e.g. Order Tracking

Found issues:

  1. Order of the heading is still wrong on
    1. cart page
    2. checkout page
    3. workplace-programs

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]

Andrey Teslya Cart, and Work Safety have been fixed for all three regional sites' respective preview themes. Your screenshot for checkout is duplicated from cart, but I am not seeing any headings order errors on checkout on my end [screen below]. In any case, most of checkout is black-boxed, and we do not have full control.

Scott Forden sorry for confusion regarding the duplicated screenshot. here is the one that there should be:

the issue is valid on CA and UK sites but not present on worldwide site. Let me know if it’s something you can fix, please?

Re-tested on Preview sites

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180450099567
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147238322313
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=145085726902

Validation of the fixed issue:

  1. Order of the heading is still wrong on
    1. cart page
    2. checkout page
    3. workplace-programs

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]

Ryan Kroening Andrej Ciho Andrey Teslya

The discrepancy between the “Express checkout” heading level in the WW, and CA checkout pages is due to the type of layout each store’s checkout is using.

WW uses the ‘Three-page’ layout option, which correctly sets the heading as an <h2>. CA uses the ‘One-page’ option that sets the heading as an <h3>.

The code is black-boxed, so we cannot make any edits. However, we can fix the heading level for the CA site by switching that store’s checkout to use the Three-page layout option.

Pete Buzzell - assigning over to you for your thoughts. Please see Scott Forden ‘s latest comment and let us know if you’d like to proceed in updating that store’s checkout to the 3-page layout option. cc Andrej Ciho
Ryan Kroening & Scott Forden - Please proceed with updating checkout on CA to match the US site (WW). Thank you
Andrey Teslya The remaining issue with the CA store’s checkout can re-tested.

Re-tested on Preview sites

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180450099567
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147238322313
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=145085726902

Validation of the fixed issues:

  • Order of the heading is still wrong on
    • CA fixed
  • UK still an issue

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]
Andrey Teslya UK site is now using Three-page checkout layout. Please check again, but you will likely need to clear browser cache.

Re-tested on Preview sites

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180450099567
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147238322313
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=145085726902

Validation of the fixed issues:

  • Order of the heading is still wrong on
    • UK

QA status: [ PASSED ]
Passing UAT. Found that CA and UK sites have a Klaviyo popup that includes an H1 and asked the CRM team to update to be H2 or lower.

item.title: [SUPS25-149] Accessibility Remediation - Color Contrast
item.description:
Issue: Some text (for example, product review counts and certain UI elements) does not meet minimum color contrast ratios. This affects users with low vision and color blindness.

WCAG References: 1.4.3 (Contrast, Minimum), 1.4.11 (Non-Text Contrast)

Recommendation: Adjust text and UI colors to ensure a 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text and 3:1 for larger headings or controls.

comments.comment: Estimate: 8h

Status: Ready for review.

PR: https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/348

Theme: SUPS25-149 Done Accessibility: Color Contrast

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180406813039
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=144981950646
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147069141129

Notes:

The following areas were addressed:

  • PDP
    • Disabled CTA background color
    • Increased font size for Size Chart description text
  • PLP/Search
    • Product card reviews count text color
    • Filters options count
  • Basic links in paragraphs
    • Managed by colors in Theme Settings
    • Example: Contact page
  • Store Locator
    • Overrides for search input placeholder text, and magnifying glass icon
  • Careers
    • Image Banner floating box background opacity increased for more contrast of foreground text
Reviewed and approved, ready for QA.

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180406813039
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=144981950646
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147069141129

Test Case:

Examine the color contrast of the site elements to ensure compliance. The following areas have been addressed to fix color contrast:

  • PDP
    • Disabled CTA background color
    • Increased font size for Size Chart description text
  • PLP/Search
    • Product card reviews count text color
    • Filters options count it doesn’t pass AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text
  • Basic links in paragraphs
    • Managed by colors in Theme Settings it doesn’t pass AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text
    • Example: Contact page
  • Store Locator
    • Overrides for search input placeholder text, and magnifying glass icon
  • Careers
    • Image Banner floating box background opacity increased for more contrast of foreground text
      • This may need to be a solid color. WCAG contrast ratio does not take opacity into account, so this may technically fail with any amount of it.

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]
Status: Updates ready for QA.

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180406813039
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=144981950646
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147069141129

Validation of the fixed issues:

  • PLP/Search
    • Filters options count - it doesn’t pass AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text
      • it’s fixed for worldwide site
      • it’s still an issue on UK and CA
  • Basic links in paragraphs
    • Managed by colors in Theme Settings - it doesn’t pass AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]

Andrey Teslya CA, and UK sites have been fixed:

  • https://superfeet.ca/collections/running?preview_theme_id=147069141129
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/collections/running?preview_theme_id=144981950646

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=180406813039
  • https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=144981950646
  • https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=147069141129

Validation of the fixed issues:

  • PLP/Search
    • Filters options count - it doesn’t pass AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text
      • it’s still an issue on UK and CA fixed

QA status: [ PASSED ]

Seeing similar improvement when I scan the page for contrast warnings using webAim WAVE chrome browser extension.aria-multiline="true", role="textbo*aria-label="Comment area, start typing to enter text."*

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Contrast ratio checker: https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/

Goal: WCAG 2.0 level AA

Test Case:

Examine the color contrast of the site elements to ensure compliance. The following areas have been addressed to fix color contrast:

  • PDP
    • Disabled CTA background color
    • Increased font size for Size Chart description text
  • PLP/Search
    • Product card reviews count text color
    • Filters options count
  • Basic links in paragraphs
    • Managed by colors in Theme Settings
    • Example: Contact page
  • Store Locator
    • Overrides for search input placeholder text, and magnifying glass icon
  • Careers
    • Image Banner floating box background opacity increased for more contrast of foreground text
      • This may need to be a solid color. WCAG contrast ratio does not take opacity into account, so this may technically fail with any amount of it.

Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

9/29/25, 9:46 AM (EST) - Pete Buzzell: Seeing similar improvement when I scan the page for contrast warnings using webAim WAVE chrome browser extension.aria-multiline="true", role="textbo*aria-label="Comment area, start typing to enter text."*

item.title: [SUPS25-146] Desire to edit and format "no results found" text
item.environment:
Beginning of a potential solution, maybe:

The "no results found" section is powered by Searchspring, outside of the reach of the Customizer, written in React, not in Shopify's Liquid. If we clear out the text and then create a Rich Text section below, within the "No results boundary", like this

https://prnt.sc/FYi6XdBsiB2X

It gets the job done but leaves too much white space between the "No results for ...." heading and the text:

https://prnt.sc/U7nrtwtIlSbN

What’s a good way to solve this?
item.summary: Desire to edit and format "no results found" text

comments.comment:
Andrej Ciho Right now, the no results page is using a resource string stored in the window object.

searchspring/superfeet/src/components/Header/Header.js - line 71 window.Resources.searchspring.results.noResultsCallToAction

This references sites/_shared/snippets/cql-head-content.liquid line 67 - noResultsCallToAction which references general.searchspring.results.no_results_cta in sites/_shared/locales/en.default.json.

If the client knows how this text should be changed/formatted we can go ahead and make that change by updating the resource string and associated styling. That would probably be the easiest way since as you mentioned, the Searchspring content comes from React and isn’t accessible via Liquid. Would this work well? It would be a small change for sure, not much dev effort required (1-2 hrs).

cc: Eddie Gemayel - if you have any ideas on this as well, feel free to share! Thanks!
Valerie Vos Great question. I asked the client if they wanted us to just change it or give them the ability to change it themselves. They chose the latter.

Andrej Ciho Thanks for the update! One idea I have is a shop level metafield. The client could update the verbiage as desired there, and for CA we could have a metafield for EN and a metafield for FR. Then we should be able to read the value in cql-head-content and add it to the window.Resources object, and from there use the value in React. Assuming that this approach works well, I’d estimate this at about 4h of effort since we’ll need to set it up and verify for all 3 sites.

Yep agreed with what Valerie said - Currently the area is editable but only in text field format. A metaobject with rich text options would probably be best if you want more semantic control over the text.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

8/19/25, 4:00 PM (EST) - Eddie Gemayel: Yep agreed with what Valerie said - Currently the area is editable but only in text field format. A metaobject with rich text options would probably be best if you want more semantic control over the text.

item.title: [SUPS25-140] Update Klaviyo Email Signup Popup
item.description:
On page load, there is a Klaviyo email signup popup that appears to the customer (both desktop and mobile):

Desktop screenshot:

This popup sometimes causes a decrease in performance scores. The image within the popup is sometimes flagged incorrectly as the largest contentful paint (LCP) element, and the popup also can cause cumulative layout shift issues on page load.

Could the popup be delayed or attached to an event other than page load, such as a certain customer interaction instead (scroll, click, etc.)?

Performance audit reports can be found on https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-138

comments.comment:
With access to the Klaviyo account, there are options for the intro offer popup that can be made directly in those settings.

  1. Timing: The timing to show the popup is currently only 1sec, where the standard range should be 5-10sec. Increasing that time would very likely have a positive impact on the LCP. I would recommend starting with a delay time of 8sec, to ensure the page has first been able to render what it needs to.
  2. Scroll: There is an option to delay the popup until the user has scrolled a certain percentage of the page. This will vary, as the length of page itself will vary, but 30% is a standard, and the default setting in Klaviyo.
  3. Number of Pages: This setting will delay the popup to show until the user has clicked through a set number of pages.
  4. Custom: Custom triggers, e.g. when a user clicks a certain button

Options #1-3 are simple, and can be adjusted by anyone with admin access to the forms. Option #4 would be more involved, and a deeper discussion about what specific triggers would be desired as a condition(s) to show the popup. An estimate would vary depending on option #4, which would inherently be more involved. For now, a general 4h estimate for options #1-3, to account for any necessary testing.

We may want to consider testing this out on the staging environment, if the Klaviyo account is connected there.
Andrej Ciho - do you mind looking over this task and Scott’s feedback and providing your recommendation on next steps please?

My recommendation would be to choose either option #2 or #3.

How to choose between the two options? Without an A/B testing framework in place, we could run option #2 for two weeks and then option #3 for two weeks and decide on the final approach based on how well each option performed.

(I exclude option #1 because I’ve seen LCP degradation even with large delays on modals such as 30 seconds.)

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: 1. Investigate Klaviyo settings/configurations and confirm if the popup behavior could be updated so that the popup no longer impacts performance.

Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

9/2/25, 4:28 PM (EST) - Andrej Ciho: My recommendation would be to choose either option #2 or #3.

How to choose between the two options? Without an A/B testing framework in place, we could run option #2 for two weeks and then option #3 for two weeks and decide on the final approach based on how well each option performed.

(I exclude option #1 because I’ve seen LCP degradation even with large delays on modals such as 30 seconds.)

item.title: [SUPS25-98] Yotpo Reviews - Change Star Color
item.description:
Superfeet rebranding UAT feedback - update star icons in Yotpo reviews section to black to be more consistent with the new branding.

Note: May be accomplished with a code change, or may need to be configured in the Yotpo dashboard.

https://app.screencast.com/Vr26NL7sjHYI7?conversation=SLQm0TBffq44PVLISpn5tR

status.description: The work item is considered finished, the resolution is correct. work items which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment:
Did some initial investigation on this and it looks like it may be better to edit this via the Yotpo widget hub than to try to do a custom code override. I don’t currently have access - added a flag for that reason.

cc: Mark Hendriks Andrej Ciho
Andrej Ciho Re-assigning this to you for now since this needs to be added to the launch checklist - seems like an item that AD would resolve. Thanks!
I added this to the launch checklist for Pete @ AD
Confirming all review stars were updated to black to be consistent with the rebrand.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: 1. Update star icon color in Yotpo Reviews to black to be more consistent with new branding.

Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

item.title: [SUPS25-66] Investigation: File drop into well-known folder on server for mobile apps
item.description:
A new mobile app is being built and the request is to get it rolled out to the public website as a notification. This requires a file to be dropped into well-known folder.

Question from Pete: How does this work on Shopify sites where we don’t have access to the folder structure?
item.summary: Investigation: File drop into well-known folder on server for mobile apps

status.description: The work item is considered finished, the resolution is correct. work items which are closed can be reopened.

comments.comment:
Pete Buzzell I confirmed with Shopify that they don’t support the .well-known folder.

A helpful article describing the common approach on Shopify is documented here: https://medium.com/umamitech-blog/smart-app-banners-for-ios-and-android-a-handmade-solution-7785d6bca205

There is also a 3rd party app in the Shopify App Store that promises to do all of this for you. It might be worth trying on staging. It’s free but only has 6 reviews so maybe not quite as established as we’d like.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Please follow the following format when adding issues

[ ] Issue Description Action Item

Expected Result:
Actual Result:
[ ] Fixed Action Item
[ ] Tested Action Item

item.title: [SUPS25-43] Superfeet Rebrand styling - logo, fonts, colors
item.description:
Acceptance Criteria

Once Superfeet provides CQL the new logo, fonts and colors, CQL will:

  1. Update these globally in a preview theme.
  2. Review the website and make adjustments to any section found to have been negatively impacted by the global change. Areas reviewed on both desktop and mobile will include
    1. Header + Navigation
    2. Footer
    3. PLP
    4. PDP
    5. Section visible on home page, work landing page, PLP and PDP
  3. Provide Superfeet a preview link on the production store
    1. This will exclude logo updates and in email notifications because those can only be changed store-wide, not in a preview. If needed, these can be updated in the staging store for review.

Notes

  1. Superfeet plans to provide the new branding artifacts by April 11th
  2. Superfeet requested to get a preview of the theme some time 3th or 4th week of April for approval.
  3. Global branding changes will be implemented on a preview theme on the production store. AD will use this theme to stage new content so CQL will exercise care to not overwrite any of their changes.
  4. Pete will implement the new branding on Aftership and Recharge
item.summary: Superfeet Rebrand styling - logo, fonts, colors

comments.comment:
Andrej Ciho Eddie Gemayel Valerie Vos Link to the Fira font and new logo.

https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fo/tx8rbo6rbvpvlk7nz1nct/AM_6u7jHpeVy2Y0KrMvJX-s?rlkey=r7zrgbksyo5lfsyokc6arwq4t&dl=0
Please do not share out new logo at this point and use at discretion. It is only being handed off to those who must access in support of the May 6^th^ date.

(I have asked AD/SF to upload the files to Basecamp as Dropbox requires signup/trial)

Valerie Vos Lucy uploaded the files in Basecamp: https://3.basecamp.com/5780542/buckets/40318328/messages/8542400882

Fonts.zip
Superfeet_Logos_4425.ai
Valerie Vos Eddie Gemayel

Valerie Vos PR is here for the fonts, with the caveat(s) i mentioned in slack below! https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/290

https://cql.slack.com/archives/C06U6B6BML6/p1745272221450569
Proxima Nova (d7f280cc-4844-46a4-a11e-c229db82488c).zip

Andrey Teslya Ready for testing!

Note, heading-alt seems less bold in the Figma than the browser; we are still getting clarification on the font differences there.

cc Mark Hendriks

Feedback from AD:

Noticing the CTA button text seem large, with a few being smaller. should all the CTA button font sizes be the same?

This is the comp - also missing the eyebrow in the preview or is that content related?

What is the font here? 

 (seems like it should be the same for the main hero)

Eddie Gemayel Valerie Vos response from Alex Miller (SF):

We have mapped styling for the expanded navigation – here is the direct Figma link, let me know if you have troubles finding it.

Thanks to the team for the global styling work – we saw the link to the preview themes come through and will review tomorrow.

Forgot to mention - We would like a drop shadow added so the expanded nav is elevated off of the page content. This does not need to be complete 5/6, we are flexible on that add.

CC: Andrej Ciho

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview:

  • US https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=176806199663
  • UK https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=142413562038
  • CA https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=142707392649

ACs tested:

See below

  1. Update these globally in a preview theme.
  2. Review the website and make adjustments to any section found to have been negatively impacted by the global change. Areas reviewed on both desktop and mobile will include
    1. Header + Navigation
    2. Footer
    3. PLP
    4. PDP
    5. Section visible on home page, work landing page, PLP and PDP
  3. Provide Superfeet a preview link on the production store
    1. This will exclude logo updates and in email notifications because those can only be changed store-wide, not in a preview. If needed, these can be updated in the staging store for review.

Found issues

  1. Header + Navigation issues are logged here: https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-77
  2. Footer issues are logged here:https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-79
  3. PLP issues are logged here:https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-80
  4. PDP issues are logged here:https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-82
  5. Homepage issues are logged here:https://cqlcorp.atlassian.net/browse/SUPS25-83

QA status: [ IN PROGRESS ]

Eddie Gemayel Andrej Ciho

New Font file (2 extra Proxima Nova - regular and bold fonts, and FiraMonoOT-Regular) and Logo files (png and svg)

Fonts New.zip
Logos.zip

Link to dropbox: https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fo/7j5jpox0jh4szw0i9t8s8/AHrdHVM79P8yun5zjPT7WeI?rlkey=0nv45gexkxyzqvzcrm2kyd68z&e=1&st=39ydgdr1&dl=0
Released today as part of rebranding - 5/6/25 at approximately 4:30 PM.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
US https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=176806199663

UK https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=142413562038

CA https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=142707392649

Figma (style guide, icons, etc) link: https://www.figma.com/files/1489015204713055823/team/1484691955289358273/Superfeet-Team?fuid=1351941933727295417

Rebrand-Aid folder/Key Pages:

https://www.figma.com/design/O10QO6lgmPs8DcfWpi2NbE/SF---WEB-Rebrand-Aid?node-id=107-34470&p=f&t=AByTExtptYovoYfd-0

Colors: https://www.figma.com/design/O10QO6lgmPs8DcfWpi2NbE/SF---WEB-Rebrand-Aid?node-id=116-16267&p=f&t=AByTExtptYovoYfd-0

item.title: [SUPS25-25] CLONE - Searchspring carousel indicator not updating
item.description:
Issue:

  • When shifting the carousel using the arrows, there were a few times where the carousel indicator did not update. It seems as though there was an extra carousel slide not represented with an indicator

Browsers:

  • Latest desktop

URL:

  • https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=136319238327
  • https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Click/tap through the carousel slides using the arrows
  2. Observe indicator does not occasionally update

Expected Results:

  • Indicator should update to match the current displaying carousel slide

Current Result:

  • The indicator does not update sometimes to match the currently displaying slide

https://app.screencast.com/pvYYKBjuXz76Q
item.summary: CLONE - Searchspring carousel indicator not updating

comments.comment:
There seems to be something weird with one of the sliders, below “for the fierce competitor” on the holiday gift page, where the indicator now works when going forward, but when hitting the back button a similar error occurs:

Edit·Delete·

Eddie Gemayel December 10, 2024 at 10:54 AM

Dustin Milam PR here https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/214

Best place to test this is the Holiday gift guide, link in the ticket. But, can be tested anywhere with a Searchspring carousel.

Edit·Delete·

Eddie Gemayel December 3, 2024 at 5:23 PM

Estimated added.

Valerie Vos PR here! https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/214 See Dustin’s screencast below to see what the issue was.

I disabled the loop on the slider if there aren’t enough slides to populate it to prevent this issue

Eddie Gemayel Code changes look great! I noticed one thing when I was looking at this - let me know if you feel like it should be a separate ticket or if you feel like it could be handled as part of this one.

So when you hover over a Searchspring product card in the carousel, a border appears around the entire card. Because the swiper carousel has a 100% width and 100% height, it looks like that border sometimes gets cut off:

I was able to resolve it by adding margin: 1px; to the .swiper-wrapper so there is enough room for the border. Maybe there’s another approach that would be better but that was just my first idea:

Valerie Vos Nice catch! I snuck that into the PR ��

Commit → https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/214/commits/c286d421a99d1103fcd3cbd0f77bf2c61cf295c6

Eddie Gemayel Nice work! Deployed that to the theme for this ticket and it looks great! PR has been approved

Matt Pokornicki This ticket is ready for QA!

Eddie Gemayel Occasionally when clicking on the previous button of a carousel the sliding animation will not occur and jump to the the next section of slides.

Also the disable looping isn’t applied to every carousel on the page, should it be?

https://app.screencast.com/OwFOqUT8iaW7t

https://app.screencast.com/vXCjTU84QKgvU

https://app.screencast.com/HUn3ONxPhNC1Y

Valerie Vos I updated the PR here to encompass the new logic for our many recommendations we have now thanks to phase 2!

https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/214

Matt Pokornicki Updated the AC so it’s less confusing, but any carousel with less than 8 slides should have their looping disabled yes �� Also the bug you posted in your last comment should be fixed

Eddie Gemayel Nice work! Everything is looking good on my side!

Matt Pokornicki This is ready for testing!

Valerie Vos Eddie Gemayel the preview link in the description of the ticket doesn’t work. It returns an error when I try to open it.

  • https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=136319238327

Could you guide me where I can test the latest updates made in this ticket?
Andrey Teslya Could you try this link please? https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Re-tested on Preview site

Theme: SUPS25-25 Done

Preview: https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Validation of the previously found issues:

  • Occasionally when clicking on the previous button of a carousel the sliding animation will not occur and jump to the the next section of slides. it’s still an issue, see video below
  • Also the disable looping isn’t applied to every carousel on the page, should it be? NaB because ‘any carousel with less than 8 slides should have their looping disabled’
  • also, I noticed that the first carousel on the page has both two visual indicators active when the only one set of slides is in view; I tried to make the view window smaller but the issue is present in that case as well

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]

Valerie Vos PR updated, commit here https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/214/commits/16d54892926def5df05002a55bdbd47ea5306427

  • ✅ This fixes the Featured Links indicators breaking when they are on the same page as an SS carousel.
  • �� The issue with the animation not playing if you click fast enough is something we will not be able to fix.

And correct, loop should be disabled for any SS carousel with less than 8 slides.

Eddie Gemayel Latest changes look good to me - just a couple things I noticed:

  1. There’s a little bit of commented code left over from the dev work on this - can it be safely removed or is it helpful to keep it there if needed?
  2. Is this branch up to date with all of the rebranding changes? I know that it was worked on before that so just wanted to double check if it needed to be updated.

Let me know what you think, if all looks good to you then this will be ready for internal QA

Thanks Valerie Vos!

  • Yep sorry about the commented out code, it should be removed now ��
  • I merged it with the latest develop and i see the new logo and colors, so i think it should be ok. Let me know!

Eddie Gemayel Sounds great! Should be all set then ��

Andrey Teslya This is ready for testing!

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview: https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Validation of the previously found issues:

  • Occasionally when clicking on the previous button of a carousel the sliding animation will not occur and jump to the the next section of slides.
    • it’s still an issue and when I click on the back arrow several times it starts to not show animation
    • also, on page load if I click on the next arrow the progress indicator doesn’t reflect it; all next clicks works properly
    • tabbing through carousel controls has weird focus
      • back arrow focus is not in place, it’s somewhere on the left from the actual arrow icon
      • next arrow focus is not accessible at all, cannot locate it via tabbing
  • also, I noticed that the first carousel on the page has both two visual indicators active when the only one set of slides is in view; I tried to make the view window smaller but the issue is present in that case as well fixed

QA status: [ FOUND ISSUES ]

Andrey Teslya Could you retest this to confirm which issues are still present since it’s been about a month since your latest findings? I took a look at this today and it seems to be working correctly:

Re-tested on Preview site

Preview: https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Valerie Vos the issue is still replicable for the a carrousel where three and more slides are available, see video below:

  • it’s still an issue and when I click on the back arrow several times it starts to not show animation
  • also, on page load if I click on the next arrow the progress indicator doesn’t reflect it; all next clicks works properly
  • tabbing through carousel controls has weird focus
    • back arrow focus is not in place, it’s somewhere on the left from the actual arrow icon
    • next arrow focus is not accessible at all, cannot locate it via tabbing

QA status: [ FAILED ]

Andrey Teslya When I click on the provided preview link, all of the carousels show only two sets of slides:

Could you please specify OS, browser, and device width to reproduce these issues? And could you please attempt to reproduce these issues in BrowserStack? I’m on a Mac, using Google Chrome, at 1477px wide.

Valerie Vos

Win11, Chrome latest, laptop, 2560x1440 screen resolution with 150% system zoom applied. I also tested it on my second monitor with 1440px viewport without system zoom applied using Firefox and the issues are the same.

Here is the video I took from Browser Stack (Safari latest in macOS Sequoia 18.4)

Andrey Teslya Thanks for the details!

Eddie Gemayel Could you take a look at this and see if you can reproduce the issues Andrey mentions? For some reason when I click the preview link, I only get two sets of carousel slides for all the carousels on the page and so I can’t reproduce the same issues. https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Hmm, last time i checked i definitely saw those issues Andrey mentioned previously. But now it seems they are gone, at least on my end?

I would love if Tonya Carlson or Pete Buzzell could take a peek at this also, and let us know what they see when they get a chance. ��

https://www.superfeet.com/pages/holiday-gift-guide?preview_theme_id=175454159215

Carousel is working as expected on the holiday gift guide page. There are 9 products in the carousel that shows up to 4 at a time (on wide screens) which causes some odd behavior in the carousel indicator when you quickly navigate back and forth between the 9 products. While odd to ecomm people, I don’t believe this will have a negative impact on the overall customer experience.
Safely merged into the upcoming release branch; awaiting deployment.
Released today 07/16/25 at 1:30 PM EST! ��

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
• Indicator dots on any Searchspring carousels update appropriately during scrolling.
  • Infinite scroll is disabled on any Searchspring carousels that have less than 8 slides to prevent bugginess
    • This is because we show 4 cards at a time - if there are less than 8 than the infinite loop breaks, it expects at least double of the number of slides you show at a time.
7/16/25, 1:36 PM (EST) - Valerie Vos: Released today 07/16/25 at 1:30 PM EST! ��

item.title: [SUPS25-24] ADA - Top-level navigation has an element with a role that hides child elements contains focusable child elements
item.description:
Powermapper scan output indicates the role=”button” on top-level navigation elements is hiding child elements from the accessibility tree.

Examples:

<summary class=' header__menu-ite...' role='button' aria-expanded='false'><a href='/collections/all' class='header__parent-link'>Shop</a></summary>
<summary class=' header__menu-ite...' role='button' aria-expanded='false'><a href='/pages/personalized-fit' class='header__parent-link'>Custom</a></summary>

Can the attribute role=”button” be removed from the navigation entirely? Between <details>, <summary>, and the <a> I believe we have enough data and context for accessibility tech to understand Superfeet.com’s navigation structure.

Powermapper detail:

An element with a role that hides child elements contains focusable child elements. WCAG 2.1 A 4.1.2 Section 508 (2017) A 4.1.2 ARIA 1.2 Presentational Children 92 pages
  This element's role marks child elements as presentational, which hides them from the accessibility tree, but some of these children are focusable, so they can be navigated to, but are not voiced in a screen reader. The role may either be specified by the role attribute or the implicit role based on the element. For example, a elements have an implicit role of link.
item.summary: ADA - Top-level navigation has an element with a role that hides child elements contains focusable child elements

comments.comment: Eddie Gemayel do you see an issue with us removing the role="button" from the nav as Pete requests? If not, can you please throw an estimate on the ticket?

After a little digging, I don’t believe anything adverse will happen by removing role=button from here.

Estimate added.

cc Mark Hendriks
Dustin Milam PR here https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/228

Eddie Gemayel Approved!

Matt Pokornicki Ready for testing

Tested on the site on the home page

The Summary element no longer has the attribute role="button" This is ready for testing.

Verified on chrome https://app.screencast.com/ihjiI0DeoctFq , safari https://app.screencast.com/pikG1JfzSWxFI
Safely merged into Release branch, awaiting deployment.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: All role="button" attributes will be removed from any navigation elements. For example, <details>, <summary>, and <a>.

Changes in this branch live in _shared and will be applied to all stores equally.

https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=175022473583

item.title: [SUPS25-23] ADA - HTML form control has no accessible name
item.description:
Powermapper has indicated that the quantity input control on PDP is failing ADA scans.

The HTML form control doesn’t have an accessible name: 

<input class='quantity__input' type='number' name='quantity' id='Quantity-template...' min='1' value='1' form='product-form-temp...'>

A label (or name) linked to the control allows screen readers to voice the label correctly when reading the control. To add a label do one of the following:

  • Use a label element with the for attribute set to the ID of the form control
  • Wrap a label element around the form control
  • Add a title attribute
  • Add an aria-label attribute
  • Add an aria-labelledby attribute
item.summary: ADA - HTML form control has no accessible name

comments.comment:
Estimate added.

cc Mark Hendriks
Dustin Milam PR here https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/229

Eddie Gemayel Approved!

Matt Pokornicki Ready for testing on linked theme.

Tested on {{ SUPS25-23 Done }}through PDP

The quantity button now has ADA compliant code. The element has attribute name="quantity" and aria-label="Quantity"

Verified on desktop chrome https://app.screencast.com/YuDQldN7aiZzH , safari https://app.screencast.com/xJbmmByWwWfDH , and firefox https://app.screencast.com/Qm0VixId8UZJt

Eddie Gemayel I noticed on the CA French example, I’m not seeing an aria-label attribute https://app.screencast.com/CRNKITD9TWUFg
So sorry bout that, linked the wrong theme. Matt Pokornicki please try now �� https://superfeet.ca/fr/products/run-support?preview_theme_id=139982897289

Thanks Eddie Gemayel

I’m seeing the aria-label attribute is present on the Ca French site as expected.

Verified on chrome https://app.screencast.com/9u9en2itfJV8J , safari https://app.screencast.com/Moj6WaLeDmGyh , and firefox https://app.screencast.com/yT3KApzfh9gEI
Safely merged into Release branch, awaiting deployment.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
https://www.superfeet.com/products/all-purpose-support-high-arch?preview_theme_id=175023685999 ← English example

https://superfeet.ca/fr/products/run-support?preview_theme_id=139982897289 ← CA french example

item.title: [SUPS25-22] ADA - Announcement Bar carousel throwing compliance error for aria-hidden=true
item.description:
Powermapper report indicates An element with aria-hidden=true contains focusable content.

This is called out on the announcement bar global element. The carousel script that rotates through the content elements in the announcement bar are being updated by the script to use aria-hidden=”true”.

The issue with this approach is that the block-section-announcement-x divs contain focusable content like links and paragraph text, but because they’re marked with aria-hidden='true', screen readers and accessibility tech will still try to read and focus on this hidden content.

Suggested approach:

  • Replace aria-hidden=”true” with tabIndex={-1} to prevent focus on hidden elements in the carousel.
  • Improve semantic markup by adding role=”region”, aria-roledescription=”carousel”, and aria-label=”announcement carousel” or something to that effect.

Flickity already adds aria-labels to the next and previous buttons.
item.summary: ADA - Announcement Bar carousel throwing compliance error for aria-hidden=true

comments.comment:
Estimate added.

cc Mark Hendriks
Dustin Milam PR here https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/230

Eddie Gemayel Excellent! Approved.

Matt Pokornicki Ready for testing

Tested on SUPS25-22 theme on the home page

For the announcement bar carousel slides, the slide(s) not displaying have the attribute tabindex="-1" to make the hidden announcements hidden from keyboard users.

Verified on safari https://app.screencast.com/yIeAVWbznPCSr , firefox https://app.screencast.com/vFr9CzsF0TYLr , and chrome https://app.screencast.com/Yhtyze9tsZIFh
Safely merged into Release branch, awaiting deployment.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: Non-visible slides in the announcement bar should not be able to be tabbed to.

Changes for this ticket live in _shared and will apply to all stores.

https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=175036891503

item.title: [SUPS25-19] ADA - Mini Cart Clickable controls should be keyboard accessible and include appropriate ARIA role
item.description:
Powermapper has indicated that “Clickable controls should be keyboard accessible.” and “Clickable controls should have an ARIA role.”

Issue is present when cart is empty. As a keyboard navigation user, I’m able to open the empty mini cart but the CTA “Continue Shopping” is not a focusable element and I’m not able to interact with it. That CTA is also missing an ARIA role attribute

Please add tabindex=0 and role=link attributes to this continue shopping CTA to bring it back into compliance.

Detail from Powermapper:

Clickable controls should be keyboard accessible.

<a onclick='this.closest('car...' class=' button  button--...'>Continue shopping</a>

Add tabindex=0 to allow keyboard users to tab to the control.

Line 1301 WCAG 2.1 A F15 Section 508 (2017) A F15

Clickable controls should have an ARIA role.

<a onclick='this.closest('car...' class=' button  button--...'>Continue shopping</a>

Controls that respond to clicks should be given an ARIA role such as role=button or role=link. divspan and a elements without an href attribute have no semantic role.

Line 1301 WCAG 2.1 A F54 Section 508 (2017) A F54
item.summary: ADA - Mini Cart Clickable controls should be keyboard accessible and include appropriate ARIA role

comments.comment:
Estimate added

cc Mark Hendriks
Valerie Vos PR here! https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/237

Eddie Gemayel Code changes look great! Just had one question on this - is the AC that if you navigate to the “Continue Shopping” button via keyboard tabbing and then press enter, should that close the mini cart? If so, I think one more tweak might need to be made to make sure we are listening for that keyboard event. Let me know what you think!

Oop - great catch! I added some JS to listen and catch the Enter keypress for this element. Check out the commit(s) here, applying to each store. https://github.com/Superfeet/sf_ShopifyB2C/pull/237/commits �� Valerie Vos

Eddie Gemayel This looks great! PR has been approved.

Matt Pokornicki This is ready for testing!

Tested on storefront through SUPS25- 19

When the mini cart is expanded and does not have any products in cart, the continue shopping CTA has the tabindex attribute equal to 0 and the role attribute set to link. These changes make the CTA ADA compliant

Verified on desktop US https://app.screencast.com/exWkjZI4DEUH6 https://app.screencast.com/5uj4kFqQDkG8S , desktop CA https://app.screencast.com/JXmFWw4HHSf2Y https://app.screencast.com/u9Dh0OULcVJzy , desktop UK https://app.screencast.com/AV5CVw01zX7qx https://app.screencast.com/Q99EogDvXiHXU
Safely merged into Release branch, awaiting deployment.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
If the cart drawer is empty, the Continue Shopping button should be able to be navigated to via tabbing.

role=link should be added to the button as well.

US Instance → https://www.superfeet.com/?preview_theme_id=175216853359

CA Instance → https://superfeet.ca/?preview_theme_id=140254740617

UK Instance → https://www.superfeet.co.uk/?preview_theme_id=141013778614
